title: Budgets, Interest, Loans, and Financial Decisions · Decision Notebook: Loan Total Cost
summary: Compare household plans, emergency savings, credit costs, and loan offers without hiding assumptions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg loan total cost increment in an explainable household decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare payment, total repayment, term, fees, and opportunity cost across loan offers.
Model compound growth by period and distinguish nominal from effective change.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Loan Total Cost evidence
steps.instruction: $1200 grows once per year at 5.0% for 2 years. Calculate the compounded value.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use P(1 + r)^t with P=1200, r=0.050, and t=2.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use P(1 + r)^t with P=1200, r=0.050, and t=2.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.
